analysis.architecture: 8-layer GPT-2-style decoder: HIDDEN=512, N_HEADS=8, FFN_DIM=2048, SEQ_LEN=128, BATCH=4

bottleneck.description: FP32 dtype forces cuBLAS to select SIMT paths on all GEMM and BMM operations. smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0.0 on both dominant kernels (ampere_sgemm_128x32_tn for linear projections, ampere_sgemm_128x128_nn for attention matmuls) confirms Tensor Cores are completely idle. A100 HMMA BF16 delivers ~312 TFLOP/s vs ~19.5 TFLOP/s FP32 SIMT — a 16x peak ratio. On these shapes a 4-6x realized improvement is conservative.

transformation.description: Cast model parameters and input tensor to torch.bfloat16. On A100 (Ampere sm80) this switches the cuBLAS dispatch from ampere_sgemm_* (FP32 SIMT) to sm80_xmma_gemm_bf16bf16_* (HMMA Tensor Core). No graph changes are needed — dtype is resolved at cuBLAS kernel selection time, below the FX IR level.
transformation.code_hint: Apply BEFORE torch.compile(). Dynamo traces dtype as a static property; changing dtype after compilation will trigger a recompile. The TransformerStack has no tied embeddings (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, out_proj, fc_up, fc_down are all independent nn.Linear modules), so the BF16 cast is safe across all parameters.
transformation.contraindications_checked: No tied embeddings detected in SelfAttention or FeedForward. LayerNorm operates element-wise and is dtype-agnostic. F.gelu path in FeedForward is BF16-compatible.

estimated_impact.speedup_model: 4x realized on GEMM shapes (512x512, 512x2048) at SEQ_LEN=128, BATCH=4 — conservative vs. 16x TFLOP/s ratio due to memory bandwidth and kernel launch overhead at this batch size

bottleneck.description: After BF16 dtype correction, the Q/K/V projections remain three separate GEMM calls each of shape (512, 512). On A100 with 108 SMs, a single (512, 512) BF16 GEMM produces approximately 0.09 waves per SM — well below the ~1.0 threshold for full SM utilization. Three separate calls serialize this under-occupancy. Fusing into a single (512, 1536) GEMM triples the wave count to ~0.27 and reduces kernel launch overhead from 3 to 1 per layer.

transformation.description: Fuse q_proj, k_proj, v_proj weight matrices into a single fused_qkv_weight buffer. Replace the three separate aten::mm calls (which all operate on the same post-LayerNorm input x) with a single aten::mm followed by a chunk/split to recover Q, K, V. This is an FX graph pass applied after torch.compile captures the graph.
transformation.location: FX pass on the compiled graph, applied after get_model_and_input() returns the BF16 model
fx_steps: # Step 1: Identify the 3 mm nodes sharing the same input activation (output of ln1).
# In the Inductor FX graph each nn.Linear(bias=False) becomes: weight_t = aten.t(get_attr(weight)); out = aten.mm(x, weight_t)
# Group mm nodes by their first argument (the shared x).
# Step 2: For each group of exactly 3 mm nodes with identical activation, proceed with fusion.
continue  # only fuse complete Q/K/V triples
# Step 3: Extract weight tensors. Each mm arg[1] is an aten.t() node wrapping a get_attr.
# The weight matrix after t() is shape (HIDDEN, HIDDEN) = (512, 512).
# Step 4: Concatenate along the output dimension to produce (1536, 512).
# After fused mm: output is (B*T, 1536); chunk(3, dim=-1) recovers Q, K, V each (B*T, 512).
# Step 5: Insert the fused mm node before the first of the three mm nodes.
# We need to transpose W_fused for mm(x, W_fused.T) convention
# Step 6: Split the fused output back into Q, K, V via chunk.
# Step 7: Replace each original mm node's uses with the corresponding chunk slice.
transformation.code_hint: Apply this FX pass via torch.compile custom backend or as a post-processing step on the captured graph. The weight order in weights[] must match the order q_proj, k_proj, v_proj as they appear in the graph to keep chunk slices semantically correct. If Inductor reorders the mm nodes, sort by the get_attr target name (q_proj, k_proj, v_proj lexicographic order) before concatenating.

estimated_impact.wave_occupancy_improvement: ~3x increase in waves per SM per attention block (from ~0.09 to ~0.27 per projection)
estimated_impact.latency_reduction_pct_of_total: 2-4 (incremental over OPT-1, primarily from reduced kernel overhead and improved SM saturation)
estimated_impact.note: OPT-2 impact is modest at BATCH=4. At BATCH>=32 the wave occupancy improves further and the fusion delivers larger gains. Primary value here is launch overhead elimination.

bottleneck.description: The attention computation in SelfAttention.forward() runs as 2 separate BMM calls plus a fused Triton softmax kernel (observed in profile: triton_per_fused__softmax_amax_mul_sub_view_3). The BMM shape is (B*N_HEADS, SEQ_LEN, HEAD_DIM) = (32, 128, 64) — extremely small. With 108 SMs on A100, a single (32, 128, 64) BMM fills only 0.296 waves per SM (from triage). Even post-BF16 this remains wave-starved. torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention routes to cuDNN FlashAttention (or the efficient-attention backend), collapsing the 2 BMMs + softmax into a single fused kernel with tiled SRAM reuse, eliminating the intermediate (B, N_HEADS, SEQ_LEN, SEQ_LEN) attention score matrix write to DRAM.

transformation.description: Replace the manual attention computation (q @ k.T * scale → softmax → @ v) in SelfAttention.forward() with a direct call to torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention. This is most cleanly done as a source-level change to transformer_stack.py, since the pattern in the FX graph has already been decomposed by Inductor's lowering passes before an FX pass would see it.
transformation.location: SelfAttention.forward() in transformer_stack.py — source level change preferred over FX pass
fx_steps: # Replace the manual attention block with F.scaled_dot_product_attention.
# out = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, attn_mask=None, dropout_p=0.0, is_causal=False)
# The scale parameter defaults to 1/sqrt(head_dim) when not provided,
# ALTERNATIVE: FX-level SDPA replacement if source change is not permissible.
# Note: Inductor decomposes softmax into exp+sum+div BEFORE the FX pass sees the graph.
# Manual graph traversal is required to find: bmm -> mul/div -> exp -> sum -> div -> bmm
# Find the two bmm nodes that share the same batch dimension and are connected through
# the softmax decomposition chain. Replace the entire subgraph with a single
# This is significantly more fragile than the source-level change.
transformation.code_hint: Source-level SDPA replacement is strongly preferred. torch.compile with mode='max-autotune' will then select the optimal FlashAttention or efficient-attention kernel automatically. On A100 with BF16 and SEQ_LEN=128, the efficient_attention backend (memory-efficient attention) or flash_attention backend will be selected by the SDPA dispatcher. The is_causal=False setting is correct here since no causal mask is applied in the original SelfAttention.

estimated_impact.kernels_eliminated: 2 BMM launches + 1 softmax launch per layer per batch → 24 kernels eliminated per forward (8 layers)
estimated_impact.dram_reduction: Eliminates the (B, N_HEADS, SEQ_LEN, SEQ_LEN) attention score write/read; at BF16 this is ~1 MB per forward across all 8 layers
estimated_impact.latency_reduction_pct_of_total: 3-6 (incremental, after OPT-1 has already reduced the absolute bmm time)
estimated_impact.wave_occupancy_note: FlashAttention kernel tile sizes are tuned for the (32, 128, 64) shape; will have higher SM utilization than the raw bmm path

bottleneck.description: Even with BF16 dtype, sm_throughput_pct for the mm operator is currently 50.8% (FP32 baseline). At BATCH=4 and SEQ_LEN=128 the effective token batch is 512 tokens — moderate for A100. max-autotune enables Triton autotuning of GEMM tile sizes specifically for the (512, 512) and (512, 2048) shapes in this model, which may select better block tilings than Inductor's default heuristic.

transformation.description: Switch torch.compile mode from 'inductor' (default) to 'max-autotune'. This enables the Triton GEMM autotuner to sweep tile configurations for the specific (M, N, K) shapes in this model and cache the optimal config.
fx_steps: # Also add TF32 allowance for any residual FP32 ops (e.g., accumulation in BF16 matmuls):
transformation.code_hint: max-autotune increases compilation time by 2-5 minutes on first run. The tuned configs are cached in the Triton/Inductor cache (~/.cache/triton/ and ~/.inductor_cache/). This is a low-risk, no-correctness-impact change.

estimated_impact.latency_reduction_pct_of_total: 2-5 (shape-dependent; gains are typically larger for non-power-of-2 or unusual K dimensions)
estimated_impact.compilation_overhead: 2-5 minutes on first compile, cached afterward

application_order.action: Add model.to(torch.bfloat16) and x.to(torch.bfloat16) in get_model_and_input()
application_order.rationale: Dtype change must be applied first. OPT-2 (QKV fusion) registers a BF16 fused weight buffer; if applied before OPT-1 it would create an FP32 fused buffer that mismatches BF16 activations at runtime.

application_order.action: Replace manual attention with F.scaled_dot_product_attention in SelfAttention.forward()
application_order.rationale: Source-level change, independent of OPT-2. Apply before OPT-2 so the graph seen by the QKV fusion pass does not include the now-eliminated BMM nodes, simplifying pattern matching.

application_order.action: Apply pass_fuse_qkv() FX pass on the compiled graph
application_order.rationale: After SDPA replacement the graph contains exactly 3 mm nodes per layer sharing the same post-LayerNorm activation. Apply QKV fusion at BF16 dtype.
Apply last — autotuning is most effective after the final graph shape is determined by OPT-2 and OPT-3.

global_notes: All ncu replay duration values are 2-5x longer than actual device execution — use for relative comparison only. The triage percentages (48.9%, 6.94%) are computed from attributed replay times and reflect operator proportions accurately, not absolute wall-clock values.

A100 SXM4 ridge point: ~250 FLOP/byte (FP32), ~156 FLOP/byte (BF16). At BATCH=4, SEQ_LEN=128, the (512, 512) GEMM arithmetic intensity is ~170 FLOP/byte — above the BF16 ridge point, confirming these are compute-bound once TC is active.

Dedup note: The profile captures layer::unique::modules_0 and propagates to modules_1 through modules_7. The 432 mm launches = 54 per layer × 8 layers (9 replay iterations × 6 linear ops per layer). Latency savings estimates above use 8x layer multiplier.

high_unattributed flag: 720 of 2176 kernels are unattributed (~33%). These are predominantly Inductor-fused Triton kernels (LayerNorm, softmax, view/transpose ops). They account for a small fraction of total time given the GEMM dominance, but should be investigated if post-optimization profiles show unexpected residual latency.
OPT-3 confidence is medium because the FX-level SDPA pattern is fragile post-Inductor-lowering. The source-level change is high-confidence; only the FX alternative carries medium confidence.
No batch norm layers exist in this model — BN folding is not applicable.
FeedForward already uses F.gelu (not tanh) — the tanh-to-GELU substitution is not applicable.
